_comment: Pele core hooks. Merged into ~/.claude/settings.json by install.sh. Edit this file (or your installed settings.json) to add project-specific hooks.

PreToolUse._comment: Block direct Edit/Write on protected branches (main / master / dev). Adjust the branch list to match your workflow.

hooks.command: b=$(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null); case "$b" in main|master|dev) printf '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"On protected branch %s, direct Edit/Write is blocked. Create a task branch first (e.g. `git checkout -b <type/scope-desc>`), then retry."}}' "$b";; esac

hooks._comment: Enforce spec-before-code: requires .specs/<slug>.md (or .specs/<slug>.skip) to exist when cwd is in a worktree. See rules/spec-before-code.md.

PostToolUse._comment: Optional: post-Codex-review prompt. Only activates when a Bash command runs codex-companion.mjs (review|adversarial-review). Harmless if you don't use the codex CLI plugin — the matcher won't fire.

hooks.command: CMD=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if echo "$CMD" | grep -Eq 'codex-companion\.mjs["'"'"'[:space:]]+(review|adversarial-review)\b'; then printf '%s' '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PostToolUse","additionalContext":"A Codex review just finished. In your next reply, do two things: (1) Briefly evaluate this review in 3-5 sentences — which are real issues, which are nitpicks or false positives, whether changes are mandatory overall; (2) Then use the AskUserQuestion tool to ask the user whether to apply the review suggestions, with 3 options (e.g. apply all / apply only critical ones / skip for now, I will decide myself). Do not start editing code first; wait for the user choice."}}'; fi

hooks._comment: Inject a 3-step reminder on every user prompt: (A) clarify requirements, (B) enter worktree if it's a new topic, (C) checkpoint after 3/7 rounds without progress.
hooks.command:
printf '%s' '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"UserPromptSubmit","additionalContext":"For code-writing requests (will land Edit/Write/NotebookEdit), handle in this order:

A) Clarify (skip if the request is already clear) — use AskUserQuestion to nail down: ① the goal ② hard constraints (where to land / stack / what NOT to touch) ③ anything you do not understand or are unsure about

B) If this is clearly a new topic ("new task / another one / next, do X"), isolate in a worktree before the first Edit, starting from latest origin/dev (do NOT use EnterWorktree(name=...) directly — it inherits current HEAD):
  git fetch origin dev
  git worktree add .worktrees/<slug> -b <type/scope-slug> origin/dev
  EnterWorktree(path=.worktrees/<slug>)
  # Project-specific: run your worktree init (e.g. generate Xcode project / npm install / build)

C) Round checkpoints: same request stuck >3 rounds with user still unsatisfied → stop and AskUserQuestion to clarify. >7 rounds → realign direction from scratch (do not auto-discard existing code).

Does not trigger for: pure Q&A / reading code / status checks / config edits. Full rules in ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md index — load on demand."}}'
